target.name: Zipline
target.founder: Keller Rinaudo Cliffton, Keenan Wyrobek, Will Hetzler

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Nuro
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

data.narrative:
Zipline is a Series G autonomous delivery drone company that has raised $600M+ to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector includes four private peers (Nuro, Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air) and no public comparables, limiting the depth of market-based valuation anchors.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($600M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement yields an implied value of $750M. Peer-median analysis is not viable here: while four same-sector peers exist, none satisfy narrow same-stage or adjacent-stage filtering requirements (minimum three comps needed), and broadening to all-sector peers would introduce excessive dispersion and reduce analytical precision.

The triangulated range derived from the single applicable method is $638M (low, representing a 15% discount to the median step-up calculation) to $862M (high, reflecting a 15% premium). The median point stands at $750M. The primary valuation uncertainty stems from limited peer benchmarking—only one quantitative method supports this range. A more robust conclusion would require either identifying same-stage comparable transaction data or securing multiples from structurally similar late-stage autonomous vehicle or logistics companies. Market conditions and capital efficiency relative to peers remain unquantified inputs.
